summary: The build failed because a unit test failed: DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion asserted that the owner version was not incremented but it was. This caused the :hibernate-core:test task to fail.
root_cause: The JUnit test org.hibernate.orm.test.version.db.DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion failed with an assertion: expected owner version not incremented (false) but was true.

evidence.reason: Shows which test failed and that it failed during the Gradle test execution.

evidence.quote: org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>
evidence.reason: The assertion failure message explains the expectation and the actual value that caused the test to fail.
Stack trace points to the exact test method and source line where the assertion failed.
Build-wide summary confirming there was exactly one failing test in the test run.
Gradle task failed because of the failing tests, which caused the overall build to fail.

evidence.quote: There were failing tests. See the report at: file:///home/runner/work/hibernate-orm/hibernate-orm/hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html
evidence.reason: Points to the test report with details for debugging the failing test.

suggested_fix: Open org.hibernate.orm.test.version.db.DbVersionTest (line 60) and inspect the assertion expecting the owner version not to be incremented. Reproduce the failure locally with Gradle and run only the failing test to iterate quickly: ./gradlew :hibernate-core:test --tests org.hibernate.orm.test.version.db.DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion. Investigate why the owner version was incremented (fix the production code or adjust the test expectation) and re-run the test until it passes; consult the test report at hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html for more details.
